summary: Corrected the GALE01 split boundaries for mnname/mnnamenew .data and applied a batch of menu-name-related symbol renames. The PR moved the mnname .data end from 0x803EE728 to 0x803EDA58 and added mnnamenew .data from 0x803EDA58 to 0x803EE728, because the author observed that data intended for mnnamenew had been incorrectly included in mnname. It also renamed several address-based or misleading functions/data symbols to behavior-oriented names, including name-list checks, sorting/input handlers, glyph variant handlers, mnnamenew entry points, and persistent name data access.
what_changed: In config/GALE01/splits.txt, mnname.c .data was shortened from start 0x803ED538 end 0x803EE728 to start 0x803ED538 end 0x803EDA58.
In config/GALE01/splits.txt, mnnamenew.c gained its own .data range: start 0x803EDA58 end 0x803EE728.
Symbols at 0x803EDA58 through 0x803EE724 were renamed from mnName_* to mnNameNew_* to match the corrected .data ownership.
GetPersistentNameData at 0x8015CC9C was renamed to PersistentNameData in symbols.txt, gmmain_lib.c, and gmmain_lib.h.

Several mnname function symbols were renamed from address placeholders or less precise names: CanCreateNewName to IsNameListFull, mnName_80237654 to CompareNameStrings, mnName_802377A8 to IsNameUnique, mnName_80237A68 to mnName_SortNames, mnName_80237F78 to mnName_ConfirmNameDeleteInput, and mnName_8023817C to mnName_MainInput.

Several mnnamenew function symbols were renamed in symbols.txt: mnNameNew_8023B3FC to mnNameNew_KeySetup, mnNameNew_8023C290 to mnNameNew_GlyphVariantInput, mnNameNew_8023C54C to mnNameNew_MainInput, mnNameNew_8023D3E8 to mnNameNew_GlyphVariantSetup, mnNameNew_8023E630 to mnNameNew_EnterFromMnName, and mnNameNew_8023E6D0 to mnNameNew_EnterFromMnCharSel.
Data symbols were given semantic names: mnName_804A0648 to mnName_NameDisplayOrder, mnName_804D4BEC to mnName_StringTerminator, and mnNameNew_804D6C0C to mnNameNew_PortInUse.
mnname.c decomp placeholder comments and mnname.h prototypes were updated to match the new symbol names.
smart_moves: Fixed the split map before deeper decompilation work, preventing mnnamenew data from remaining incorrectly attributed to mnname.
Used the boundary address 0x803EDA58 consistently: it became the new end of mnname .data and the start of mnnamenew .data.
Renamed symbols in all required places for declared functions: symbols.txt, source placeholder comments, and headers stayed synchronized.
Converted several address-based names into behavior-oriented names while preserving the mnName/mnNameNew prefixes for menu UI-specific routines.
Renamed data ownership along with split ownership, which makes future data references and TU attribution less misleading.
decomp_lessons: When .data appears to contain objects for the following translation unit, check the split boundary rather than assuming all contiguous data belongs to the previous TU.
Split fixes should be reflected in both config/GALE01/splits.txt and config/GALE01/symbols.txt so object names agree with section ownership.
For undecompiled functions represented by placeholder comments, update the placeholder name at the same time as the symbol and header declaration.
A symbol rename PR can still be important decomp infrastructure work when it corrects TU attribution and makes future matching easier to reason about.
Semantic names like IsNameListFull, CompareNameStrings, IsNameUnique, MainInput, and KeySetup provide useful hypotheses for later matching without changing function bodies.
naming_conventions: Author stated a loose convention: keep the mn<TU NAME>_ prefix for functions/data directly related to the UI and drop it for ones that are not.
UI handlers retained TU-prefixed names such as mnName_MainInput, mnName_ConfirmNameDeleteInput, mnNameNew_MainInput, mnNameNew_GlyphVariantInput, and mnNameNew_GlyphVariantSetup.
Non-UI helper-style names dropped the mnName prefix, as with IsNameListFull, CompareNameStrings, IsNameUnique, IsNameValid, CreateNameAtIndex, GetNameText, and GetNameCount.
Corrected mnnamenew-owned data symbols to use the mnNameNew_ prefix after moving their .data range into mnnamenew.c.
Data symbols can be made more searchable with role names, as shown by mnName_NameDisplayOrder, mnName_StringTerminator, and mnNameNew_PortInUse.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use section ownership and raw addresses to diagnose split errors: mnname .data now ends at 0x803EDA58 and mnnamenew .data begins there.
When an apparent TU split is wrong, reassign the split range and rename all affected data symbols so future disassembly/decomp references align with the owning source file.
Batch renames that only touch symbol names, prototypes, and placeholder comments are low-risk when function addresses and sizes remain unchanged.
The PR did not provide evidence of new C matching tricks, instruction-level changes, or function-body rewrites beyond renaming one implemented function declaration/definition.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body explained the rationale: mnname and mnnamenew .data seemed incorrectly split because data intended for mnnamenew had 8 bytes of data only present in mnname.
No reviewer-requested changes or naming objections were evidenced in the provided context.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that decompile or match mnName_MainInput, mnNameNew_MainInput, or mnNameNew_KeySetup using these renamed symbols.
Check whether PersistentNameData should eventually be renamed to a verb phrase if its behavior is confirmed to return a pointer rather than represent data directly.
Audit adjacent menu translation units for similar .data boundary mistakes where data belonging to the next TU was assigned to the previous one.
Search for references to mnNameNew_803EDA58, mnNameNew_803EDA7C, mnNameNew_803EDCE4, and mnNameNew_803EE720 to confirm all mnnamenew data users are in the corrected TU.
